reasoning: The question asks if there will be more than 10 times the 30-day average of protests (from the year prior to June 22, 2025) in any of the 30-day periods preceding four resolution dates. We know the recent 30-day average is approximately 202 (which makes the base value ~203). Therefore, for the question to resolve YES, we would need to see over 2,030 protests in a single 30-day window (roughly 67 protests per day for an entire month). This baseline of 202 average protests inherently incorporates the immense wave of protests that struck Bangladesh in July/August 2024, representing an already significantly elevated tempo of political unrest. Sustaining 10 times this elevated rate in a single month would require a total breakdown of civil society and an unprecedented explosion of protests at a scale far exceeding even the 2024 revolution that ousted Sheikh Hasina. Since such a 10x multiplier on an already elevated baseline is virtually impossible under ACLED's methodology, the probability is extremely low. If ACLED changes its recording methodology to capture more granular events, they typically update historical data retroactively, which would increase the baseline proportional to the current month—meaning the 10x ratio remains highly protective against mere methodological changes. Probabilities slightly increase over time to account for growing but still marginal uncertainties about far-future political dynamics, keeping values strictly close to 0 given the astronomically high sheer threshold required.
